message.content:
Adversarially verify this reverse-engineered architecture claim. Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot
confirm it from a PRIMARY source (paper, repo, official doc) or from the artifact text itself.

SYSTEM: WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 (浩鲸科技 / WhaleCloud, Nanjing; DeepResearch Bench 100-task submission). Rank 2 on our GPT-5.5 RACE board at 54.78.
HAS PAPER: false
ARCHITECTURE CLAIMED: PUBLISHED RECORD: essentially NOTHING. Four searches (EN + zh: 深度研究/智能体/报告生成, arXiv, GitHub, DRB leaderboard, HF Spaces) returned no paper, no repo, no tech report, no blog post, no HF card for a WhaleCloud deep-research agent. WhaleCloud (浩鲸科技, iwhalecloud.com) is a real telecom BSS/OSS + data-intelligence vendor, and "DocChain" IS a real WhaleCloud product name — but for unstructured-document→structured-knowledge extraction (CSDN post blog.csdn.net/whalecloud/article/details/140456681), NOT a research agent. The link between that product name and this DRB submission is PLAUSIBLE BUT UNVERIFIED. The "_0612" suffix suggests a dated submission build. Everything below is reverse-engineered from the 100 artifacts, not from any doc.

ARCHITECTURE INFERRED FROM ARTIFACT (100/100 reports read/measured):

The system self-describes its own contract inside the reports. Task 3, methodology section, verbatim: "所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出" — "every conclusion carries a DERIVATION CHAIN and a CONFIDENCE LABEL; unverified points are explicitly listed." That single sentence is the whole design: it does NOT span-verify. It permits cross-source inference and pays for it with (a) a visible reasoning chain, (b) a confidence rating, (c) an explicit gap ledger.

Stages I can evidence:
1. PLAN / DECOMPOSE. Reports carry a "背景与范围界定 / Background and Scope" chapter (20+16 occurrences) that states an explicit scope contract: 地理范围/时间范围/公司范围/信息维度 (geography, time window, entity set, information dimensions). A stored sub-question list must exist, because a later chapter is literally titled "研究结论与子问题回应 / Research Conclusions and Responses to Sub-Questions" (~13 docs) or "…Responses to Core Questions."
2. RETRIEVE + COVERAGE CHECK. There is a machine-shaped gap ledger: "信息缺口与未完成项 / Information Gaps and Unfinished Items" (~27 docs), rendered as a TABLE with columns 编号 | 缺口描述 | 重要性 | 建议验证方式 (ID | gap | importance | suggested verification route; e.g. G1..G10). You can only emit that if you tracked planned sub-questions against retrieved evidence and logged the misses. This is a coverage loop, not a narrative flourish.
3. OUTLINE. Outlines are ARGUMENT-shaped, not topic-shaped, and are derived from the user's question. Task 42 title: "现状诊断、制约机制与政策路径" (diagnosis → constraint mechanism → policy path) — the user asked three things (how much time now? what factors? what policy?) and chapters 2/3/4 map 1:1 onto them.
4. DRAFT (section-scoped, likely parallel). Cross-chapter reference density is LOW: 0.20/10kw vs cellcog-max 1.04. Sections are self-contained. Depth is 3 levels: mean 13 H2 + 37 H3 per report.
5. FORCED SYNTHESIS PASS. Because sections are siloed, integration is done in DEDICATED slots: a "因素交互机制" ("how the five factors interlock") subsection inside the body, plus a whole "Key Insights / 关键洞察 / 洞察发现" chapter (~48/100 docs), plus "综合分析与独到洞察."
6. RENDER. Mermaid mindmaps in 27/100 docs; ★★★★★ evidence-strength ratings in 37/100.

Scale: 10,051 words mean (median 9,638) — HALF of cellcog-max (20,810) yet only 1.0 pt behind it. It is the most efficient report on the board per word.
COMPOSITION METHOD CLAIMED: THE ANSWER: it composes section-scoped and then runs a SEPARATE, DEDICATED SYNTHESIS PASS whose only job is to emit cross-source inferences. Synthesis is not a byproduct of good writing — it is a named pipeline stage with its own output slot.

The "Key Insights" chapter is a numbered list of 5-6 insights, each of which is a COINED, NAMED MECHANISM in bold followed by a causal chain. Task 21 (AI in education), verbatim:
- "**落地悖论**：AI教育应用中技术成熟度最高的领域（如智能测评）恰好是伦理争议最大的领域，而伦理风险较低的领域（如校园管理）却因商业模式不清晰而落地缓慢" — "the deployment paradox: the domains with the highest technical maturity are exactly the domains with the greatest ethical controversy." That is a CROSS-TABULATION of two facts from two different sources. No single span states it. It is unverifiable by extraction — and it is precisely what scores insight.
- "**数字鸿沟的算法加速效应**：训练数据偏向优势群体→算法为优势群体提供更精准服务→优势群体获得更大提升→差距进一步扩大" — a 4-link causal chain written with literal arrows.

THE ARROW IS THE SIGNATURE. WhaleCloud emits 41.7 "→" per 10k words — #1 on the board, 4x cellcog-max (11.1), 5x bodhi (7.7), 140x gemini/openai (0.3). And 3,084 of 4,128 arrows are in PROSE/bullets, not tables. The arrow IS the "derivation chain" the methodology promises. It is how the system licenses an inference it cannot cite: it shows the mechanism instead of a source span.

CONFLICT HANDLING = DEFINITIONAL RECONCILIATION, NOT HEDGING. It almost never writes "sources disagree" (conflict-explicit only 1.3/10kw). Instead it reframes disagreement as 口径 (measurement basis / definitional scope) and TABULATES the answer under each basis. Task 7 exec summary: a 4-row table 口径 | 年均减少额 | 占比 | 影响程度, from "仅房地产五税 → 显著但可控" up to "房地产相关收入合计 → 重大且深远". Conflict becomes a finding rather than a caveat.

ANSWER-FIRST EXEC SUMMARY. 86/100 reports open with an Executive Summary that leads with a bolded verdict token — "核心回答 / 核心结论 / Bottom Line" (1.06/10kw, highest on the board alongside lunon). Then it REFRAMES: task 7's "关键发现：税收减少仅是'冰山一角'——土地出让金缩减规模是税收减少规模的近8倍" (the user asked about taxes; the report says taxes are the tip of the iceberg, land-sale revenue is 8x bigger). It answers past the literal question. That is an insight-scoring move and an instruction-following move at once.

REVISION/CRITIC: no direct proof of a critic loop. But three artifacts require state carried ACROSS stages and cannot come from one pass: the gap ledger (planned vs answered), the "responses to sub-questions" chapter (stored decomposition), and the Key Insights chapter (reads all body chapters). Mark the critic itself as UNVERIFIED; the multi-stage pipeline is well-evidenced.
EVIDENCE CITED: ARTIFACT (all measured by me this session, 100/100 reports):
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl (100 rows: id, prompt, article)
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612/race_result.txt — Comp .5514 / Insight .5533 / IF .5485 / Read .5248 / Overall .5478
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/scores/WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612/raw_results.jsonl — per-task RACE scores (100 rows)
- /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv — citation_accuracy and effective_citations are "-" for ALL 10 systems, i.e. citations are NOT scored on this board.
Measured cross-system (per 10k words): arrows "→" WhaleCloud 41.7 / cellcog 11.1 / bodhi 7.7 / lunon 17.1 / gemini 0.3 / openai 0.3. Inline-cite attachment to prose numeric claims: WhaleCloud 7.9% / sourcery 51.3% / dalpha 34.4% / bodhi 29.4% / gemini 17.9% / cellcog 15.9%. Cross-chapter refs: WhaleCloud 0.20 vs cellcog 1.04. Confidence labels: WhaleCloud 5.55, ★ ratings in 37/100 docs, mermaid in 27/100.
Key verbatim quotes: task 3 methodology ("所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出"); task 3 gap table (G1–G10, cols 编号|缺口描述|重要性|建议验证方式); task 7 exec summary 口径 table; task 21 Key Insights (落地悖论 / 数字鸿沟的算法加速效应); task 42 outline (现状诊断、制约机制与政策路径).
Section-title census across 100 docs: Executive Summary 55 + 执行摘要 31 (=86); Key Insights 29 + 关键洞察 14 + 洞察发现 5 (=48); Information Gaps 16+4+3 + 信息缺口 variants (~27); References/参考来源/参考文献 (~91).

PUBLISHED RECORD (searched, found nothing):
- https://www.iwhalecloud.com/ — WhaleCloud corporate site; no research-agent product page found.
- https://blog.csdn.net/whalecloud/article/details/140456681 — the ONLY "DocChain" primary source: WhaleCloud's unstructured→structured document tech for resource-data analysis. NOT a deep-research agent. Link to this submission is UNVERIFIED.
- https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench and https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.11763 (DeepResearch Bench, RACE+FACT) — the benchmark; no WhaleCloud entry surfaced.
- HF Spaces Ayanami0730/DeepResearch-Leaderboard returned HTTP 401; muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard returned an unloaded shell. Could NOT confirm a public leaderboard listing for WhaleCloud either way.

Check: does the cited source actually say this? Is it current (2026)? Is an "architecture" being inferred from
output text and then stated as fact? Inference from artifacts is LEGITIMATE but must be LABELLED as inference,
not presented as documented design. Flag any place that line is crossed.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,glob,statistics
def words(t):
    # count CJK chars + latin words
    cjk=len(re.findall(r'[一-鿿]',t))
    lat=len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]+',t))
    return cjk+lat
for f in sorted(glob.glob('*.jsonl')):
    rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open(f)]
    arts=[r.get('article','') for r in rows]
    tot=sum(words(a) for a in arts)
    ar=sum(a.count('→') for a in arts)
    print(f"{f:45s} n={len(rows):3d} meanw={tot/len(rows):8.0f} arrows/10kw={ar/tot*10000:6.2f} totarrow={ar}")
EOF

cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re
rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl')]
print(rows[0].keys())
def cnt(pat):
    return sum(1 for r in rows if re.search(pat, r['article']))
for p in ['Executive Summary','执行摘要','Key Insights','关键洞察','洞察发现','Information Gap','信息缺口','背景与范围','Background and Scope','子问题','Sub-Question','mermaid','★★★★★','置信度','参考']:
    print(f"{p:25s} {cnt(p):3d}")
# headings depth
import statistics
h2=[len(re.findall(r'^## ',r['article'],re.M)) for r in rows]
h3=[len(re.findall(r'^### ',r['article'],re.M)) for r in rows]
print('mean H2',statistics.mean(h2),'H3',statistics.mean(h3))
EOF

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re
rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl')]
A=[r['article'] for r in rows]
def u(*pats): return sum(1 for a in A if any(re.search(p,a,re.I) for p in pats))
print('exec union', u('Executive Summary','执行摘要'))
print('insights union', u('Key Insights','关键洞察','洞察发现','独到洞察'))
print('gaps union', u('Information Gap','信息缺口','Unfinished Item'))
print('star any ★', sum(1 for a in A if '★' in a))
print('methodology sentence docs', sum(1 for a in A if '所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注' in a))
print('推导链路 docs', sum(1 for a in A if '推导链路' in a))
print('置信度 docs', sum(1 for a in A if '置信度' in a or 'onfidence' in a))
# arrows in table rows vs not
tot=pr=0
for a in A:
    for line in a.split('\n'):
        c=line.count('→')
        tot+=c
        if not line.strip().startswith('|'): pr+=c
print('arrows total',tot,'non-table',pr)
# conflict explicit
print('核心回答/核心结论/Bottom Line docs', u('核心回答','核心结论','Bottom Line'))
EOF

cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json,re,collections
rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open('WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl')]
c=collections.Counter()
n=0
for r in rows:
    a=r['article']
    m=re.search(r'^#{1,3}.*(Executive Summary|执行摘要).*$',a,re.M|re.I)
    if not m: continue
    n+=1
    seg=a[m.end():m.end()+400]
    b=re.findall(r'\*\*(.{2,20}?)\*\*',seg)
    c[b[0] if b else '(none)']+=1
print('exec-heading docs',n)
for k,v in c.most_common(20): print(f'{v:3d} {k}')
EOF

Based on the search results, I found information about DeepResearch Bench and related deep research agents, but I did not find any specific information about "WhaleCloud" or "DocChain" in the search results. Here's what I found:

## DeepResearch Bench

DeepResearch Bench is a benchmark consisting of 100 PhD-level research tasks, each meticulously crafted by domain experts across 22 distinct fields. Deep Research Agents work by autonomously orchestrating multistep web exploration, targeted retrieval, and higher-order synthesis, transforming vast amounts of online information into analyst-grade, citation-rich reports.

### Evaluation Methodology

DeepResearch Bench includes two evaluation frameworks: RACE (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation framework with Dynamic Weighting) for evaluating the quality of generated research reports, and FACT (Framework for Factual Abundance and Citation Trustworthiness) for assessing information retrieval effectiveness and citation accuracy.

### Real-World Grounding

The benchmark was developed by analyzing 96,147 raw user queries from interactions with web search-enabled LLM Chatbot, and the resulting distribution guided the benchmark construction to ensure the 100 tasks maintain the same topical balance as observed in real-world usage patterns.

However, **I did not find any information about WhaleCloud or DocChain** in the search results. If you're looking for information about those specific systems, they may not be covered in the available search results, or they may be relatively new or specialized projects.

input.reasoning:
WHAT I DID: re-measured all quantitative claims directly against /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612.jsonl (100 rows), /scores/WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612/race_result.txt, /leaderboard_gpt55.csv, and the 9 sibling system .jsonl files; grepped every verbatim quote; ran 2 fresh web searches (EN + zh) for a published record.

CONFIRMED (exact reproduction):
- Scores: RACE .5514/.5533/.5485/.5248/.5478; leaderboard shows cellcog-max 55.78 vs WhaleCloud 54.78 (=1.0 pt gap); citation_accuracy + effective_citations are "-" for ALL 10 systems (citations genuinely not scored on this board). 100 rows.
- Mean words 10,051 vs cellcog-max 20,810 (~half). Mean 13.15 H2 / 36.9 H3.
- Arrows: 4,128 total; 41.1 per 10kw — #1 on the board by a wide margin (cellcog 10.5, lunon 14.3, bodhi 7.5, sourcery 8.3, gemini 0.26, openai 0.13, grok 0). 3,084 of 4,128 arrows are on non-table lines — reproduces the claim exactly. The "arrow is the signature" finding is solid.
- Executive Summary heading in exactly 86/100. mermaid in 27/100. ★ present in 37/100 (★★★★★ specifically in 27/100).
- All five verbatim anchors exist where claimed: task 3 methodology sentence "所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出" and gap-table columns 编号/缺口描述/建议验证方式; task 7 口径 + "冰山一角"; task 21 "落地悖论" and "数字鸿沟的算法加速效应"; task 42 "现状诊断、制约机制与政策路径".
- Published record: my searches also return nothing — no paper, repo, HF card, or DRB leaderboard entry for a WhaleCloud deep-research agent. The zh search surfaced only WhaleCloud's "鲸智百应" enterprise AI OS, unrelated to DRB. The claim's own UNVERIFIED labels on the DocChain-product link and on the critic loop are appropriate and honest.

WHERE THE LINE IS CROSSED (why PARTIAL, not CONFIRMED):
1. n=1 promoted to "the whole design". The methodology sentence appears in exactly ONE of 100 reports (task 3). "推导链路" appears in 11/100; "置信度" in 38/100. Calling that single sentence "the system self-describes its own contract" and "that single sentence is the whole design" is a 1% observation stated as a system-wide contract.
2. Pipeline stated as fact, not inference. "Stages I can evidence", "it runs a SEPARATE, DEDICATED SYNTHESIS PASS", "Synthesis ... is a named pipeline stage with its own output slot", "the multi-stage pipeline is well-evidenced" — none of this is separable from a SINGLE-PASS generation following a fixed report TEMPLATE (prompt says: include background/scope, gap ledger, key insights, confidence labels, references). The claim's supporting argument is explicitly wrong: it says the gap ledger / sub-question chapter / insights chapter "require state carried ACROSS stages and cannot come from one pass". A one-pass long-form generation has the plan, the sub-questions and the whole body in its own context window and can trivially emit all three. Output section headings cannot discriminate pipeline stages from a template. This is inference from output text presented as documented design, with no primary source.
3. Overstated: "86/100 reports open with an Executive Summary that leads with a bolded verdict token — 核心回答/核心结论/Bottom Line". 86/100 have an Exec Summary heading, but 41 of those 86 have NO bold token at all in the first 400 chars after the heading, and the three named tokens occur in only 16/100 docs total. The actual dominant lead token is 核心判断 (15). The verdict-first behavior is real but roughly a quarter of reports, not 86.
4. Numeric drift (minor, none rank-changing): lunon arrows 14.3 not 17.1; cellcog 10.5 not 11.1; bodhi 7.5 not 7.7; "140x gemini/openai" is actually ~158x/~316x; "Key Insights chapter ~48/100" undercounts (union of Key Insights/关键洞察/洞察发现/独到洞察 = 62/100); "Information Gaps ~27" undercounts (union = 43/100; 信息缺口 alone = 27).
5. Not reproduced (method-dependent, neither confirmed nor refuted): cross-chapter ref density 0.20 vs 1.04; inline-cite attachment 7.9% vs sourcery 51.3%; "confidence labels 5.55".

BOTTOM LINE: the artifact measurements and the behavioral signature (arrow-dense derivation chains in prose, 口径-based definitional reconciliation instead of hedging, coined-mechanism insights, gap ledger, answer-first summary, half the words of the leader for 1 pt less) are real and largely reproduce. The ARCHITECTURE — plan/retrieve/outline/parallel-draft/dedicated-synthesis-pass/render as discrete pipeline stages — is an unfalsifiable inference from output structure, has zero primary-source support, and is written in fact voice. Downgrade every stage claim to "output-consistent with", and drop the "cannot come from one pass" argument entirely.
input.corrected:
WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 (DRB 100-task submission; Rank 2 on our GPT-5.5 RACE board at 54.78).

PUBLISHED RECORD: none. Independently re-searched (EN + zh) — no paper, repo, tech report, blog, HF card, or DRB leaderboard entry for a WhaleCloud deep-research agent. "DocChain" is a real WhaleCloud product name but for unstructured-document→structured-knowledge extraction; its link to this submission is UNVERIFIED. WhaleCloud's public AI product line is "鲸智百应", not a research agent. EVERYTHING BELOW IS INFERRED FROM OUTPUT TEXT ONLY. No stage boundary below is observed; all of it is equally consistent with a SINGLE-PASS generation driven by a fixed report template.

MEASURED, VERIFIED FACTS (100/100 reports, all reproduced this session):
- Scale: 10,051 mean words (half of cellcog-max's 20,810) for 1.0 RACE pt less. Most score-per-word on the board. 13.2 H2 / 36.9 H3 mean.
- ARROW SIGNATURE (the strongest finding): 41.1 "→" per 10k words, #1 on the board — 4x cellcog-max (10.5), 3x lunon (14.3), 5x bodhi (7.5), >150x gemini (0.26) / openai (0.13). 3,084 of 4,128 arrows are in prose/bullets, not tables. The arrow carries multi-link causal chains in running text.
- Template slots present: Executive Summary heading 86/100; an insights chapter (Key Insights/关键洞察/洞察发现/独到洞察) 62/100; an information-gap chapter (信息缺口/Information Gaps) 43/100, sometimes rendered as a G1..Gn table with columns 编号|缺口描述|重要性|建议验证方式; mermaid mindmaps 27/100; ★ evidence ratings 37/100 (★★★★★ specifically 27/100); 置信度 mentioned in 38/100.
- Citations are NOT scored on this board (citation_accuracy = "-" for all 10 systems), which is why an inline-cite-light, inference-heavy style is not penalized.

STYLE MOVES (verified verbatim, and these are what plausibly score Insight):
- Coined, named mechanisms in the insights chapter, e.g. task 21: "落地悖论" (highest technical maturity coincides with greatest ethical controversy — a cross-tabulation of two facts from two sources, stated in no single span) and "数字鸿沟的算法加速效应" written as a literal 4-link arrow chain.
- Conflict handled as definitional reconciliation, not hedging: reframes disagreement as 口径 (measurement basis) and tabulates the answer under each basis (task 7's 4-row 口径|年均减少额|占比|影响程度 table). Conflict becomes a finding, not a caveat.
- Verdict-first exec summaries that REFRAME the question (task 7: taxes are "冰山一角"; land-sale revenue is ~8x bigger). NOTE: only ~25 of the 86 exec summaries actually lead with a bolded verdict token, most often 核心判断 (15 docs); 核心回答/核心结论/Bottom Line together appear in just 16/100. Verdict-first is a real but MINORITY pattern, not 86/100.
- One report (task 3, 1/100) states a methodology contract verbatim: "所有结论均附推导链路和置信度标注，未验证点已明确列出". This is a single self-description in a single output, NOT a system spec — do not generalize it to the pipeline. ("推导链路" appears in only 11/100 reports.)

ARCHITECTURE: UNVERIFIED / UNKNOWN. The reports are output-consistent with plan→retrieve-with-coverage-check→argument-shaped outline→section-scoped drafting→dedicated synthesis slot→render. But a gap ledger, a "responses to sub-questions" chapter, and a whole-body insights chapter can all be emitted by one pass with the plan and body in a single context window — so they are NOT evidence of cross-stage state, and the claim that they "cannot come from one pass" is false. Both the critic loop AND the multi-stage pipeline should be marked UNVERIFIED. What IS defensible: this system trades span-verifiability for visible mechanism (arrows), and it wins per-word on a board that does not score citations.
